    Global Search Dialog
      1. About RWJF
      2. Impact Investments

      Impact Investments

      The Robert Wood Johnson Foundation uses grantmaking, convening, advocacy, evidence building, and impact investing to address barriers to health and wellbeing.

      Our impact investments—deposits, loans, equity investments, and guarantees—help bring capital to communities that have been overlooked by the mainstream financial system, seek to address the root causes of health inequities, and help lead the way toward a future where health is no longer a privilege but a right.

      We envision flourishing communities where everyone has a fair and just opportunity to thrive, including access to clean, safe drinking water and safe, stable housing. Since 2010, RWJF has committed $625 million to impact investments to help advance this vision alongside other public and private-sector investors.

      We set a goal to attract or “leverage” $1 billion for health and racial equity from other investors like banks, insurance companies, and private investors by 2025. We are thrilled to have surpassed that goal in 2024. This is a powerful example of philanthropy extending its impact by harnessing capital from other investors and helping direct it toward issues and places that need it most.

      Our Strategies

      Our impact investments focus on three areas:

      Advancing Racial Equity  Through Homeownership Preservation and Economic Inclusion:

      Reducing the racial wealth gap by increasing and preserving Black and Brown homeownership as a lever to strengthen financial wellbeing and fostering intergenerational wealth transfer. Learn more about this strategy.

      Strengthening the Community Development Finance System:

      Strengthening the community development finance system with a focus on communities that historically have experienced a lack of investment, especially communities with low incomes and communities of color. Learn more about this strategy.

      Program-Directed Investments:

      Complementing grantmaking portfolios across the Foundation with program-linked investments, such as to improve a community’s water infrastructure and investing in New Jersey. Learn more about this strategy.

      Featured Content

      How We Drive Capital to Communities that Need it Most

      RWJF uses impact investments to advance racial equity, strengthen the community development finance system, and complement our grantmaking work, helping ensure everyone, no matter their ZIP code, has what they need to live their healthiest lives.

      How We Drive Capital to Communities that Need It Most

      RWJF believes that impact investments have the power to help us achieve our goal of reaching a future where health is no longer a privilege, but a right. Key to this goal is ensuring everyone has the resources and financial stability that enable them to thrive. 

      Our strategies support efforts that can make this a reality. While our work continues to evolve, our mission remains clear. Learn more about each of our three overarching strategies, and dive deeper into some of our areas of work, in these explainers:

      • Advancing Health and Racial Equity Through Homeownership Preservation
      • Strengthening the Community Development Finance System
      • Using Program-Linked Investments to Support Our Grantmaking Goals
      • Preserving Homeownership by Strengthening Homeowners’ Insurance Protections
      • Government Subsidies, Public Policies, and Programs are Key to Attracting Private Capital for Public Good
      Editor's Pick

      Opportunities for Impact Investing to Champion Reproductive Care After Dobbs

      The Bridgespan Group shares research results to identify what opportunities exist for timely impact investments in the reproductive field.

      Learn more

      Case Studies

      Brief

      Community Foundations Spur Local Development for Health and Wellbeing

      Individual loans are supporting community development and economic mobility through small business financing, increased access to affordable housing, and reduced costs of homeownership.
      Equitable Community Development Community Power
      Brief

      Building Generational Wealth and Greater Wellbeing through New and Better Homes

      A $10 million loan from RWJF will support Habitat Capital’s pilot enterprise loan product and research into the feasibility and development of a potential home repair loan program.
      Housing
      Brief

      Fostering Homeownership Stability, Creating a Model for Other CDFIs

      This loan will allow Sustainable Neighborhoods, a national community development financial institution (CDFI) subsidiary of the Center for NYC Neighborhoods, to preserve and protect homeownership and to continue expanding its work outside of New York City.
      Social Determinants of Health Housing
      View all

      Investing in New Jersey

      We drive investment to our home state of New Jersey to strengthen the community development finance system. By doing so, we hope to help communities that lack the essentials that help residents thrive tap the power of financial tools to develop them.

      Learn more about our N.J.-focused investments and related grants

      Advancing Health Equity in New Jersey

      We use all the tools in our toolbox to help New Jersey become a place where everyone can live their healthiest possible lives.

      Explore our vision for New Jersey

      Featured Resources

      Impact at Work: An Examination of Corporate Impact Investing Strategies and Their Durability

      The goal of this report is to examine corporate impact investing, or the leveraging of a corporate’s balance sheet or investment capital to advance positive social, economic and environmental outcomes alongside financial considerations. 

      Read the report

      Impact Investing Opportunities to Advance Water, Health, and Equity

      There is an opportunity for philanthropic impact investors to align investment strategies toward an equitable water future. An RWJF-funded report from the Environmental Policy Innovation Center shares three recommendations that offer strategies for impact investors to support and finance improvements in water infrastructure.

      Read the report

      Centering Racial Equity in Municipal Bonds

      This framework was developed to support municipal bond issuers who want to leverage markets as a catalyst for changing racially inequitable conditions in their communities.

      Read the report

      2025 Impact Investment Yearly Summary

      In 2025, RWJF made more than $24 million in loans, equity investments, and guarantees to six organizations across the U.S.

      Read the 2025 summary

      2024 Impact Investment Yearly Summary

      In 2024, alongside a growing number of co-investors, RWJF made more than $50 million in loans, equity investments, and guarantees to nine organizations across the U.S. 

      Read the 2024 summary

      2023 Impact Investment Yearly Summary

      In 2023, alongside a growing number of co-investors, RWJF made $48 million in loans, equity investments, and guarantees to eight organizations across the U.S.

      Read the 2023 summary

      2022 Impact Investment Yearly Summary

      In 2022, RWJF was proud to make strategic impact investments in organizations in collaboration with co-investors across the U.S. as part of our commitment to improving health and racial equity. 

      Read the 2022 summary

      From the Blog

      Blog Post

      Using Impact Investments to Boost Economic Growth in Latino Communities

      Those with expertise, lived experience, and cultural and social understanding can drive progress that helps more Latino communities thrive.
      Equitable Community Development Structural Racism
      Blog Post

      Who Finances Healthy Communities?

      When people establish roots, make friendships, raise families, and participate in activities like volunteering or mentoring, a place becomes a community.
      Equitable Community Development
      Blog Post

      How We’re Investing to Advance Racial Equity

      RWJF is making impact investments in organizations across the U.S. to address the racial wealth gap by expanding home and business ownership and good jobs in communities of color.
      Health Disparities Structural Racism
      View all

      Related Content

      Funding to Center Equity in Municipal Bonds

      The $4 trillion municipal bond market is one of the largest pools of private investment capital flowing into America's states and localities, shaping the built environment in communities across the country and directly impacting health and equity. In a first-of-its-kind effort, a $4 million grant from RWJF aims to empower communities across America to leverage municipal bond market investments to disrupt historic systemic racial inequities.

      Download the white paper

      Stay informed

      Thank you. You are now subscribed.

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.
